text: Lok Sabha Election 2024 (18th). NDA: 293 seats (BJP 240, TDP 16, JDU 12, Shiv Sena 7, others). INDIA alliance: 234 seats (INC 99, SP 37, TMC 29, DMK 22, others). Others: 16. Turnout: 65.79%. PM Modi won 3rd term.
Lok Sabha Election 2019 (17th). NDA: 353 seats (BJP 303 — highest since 1984). UPA: 91 (INC 52). Others: 99. Turnout: 67.4% (highest ever). PM Modi re-elected. Key: Pulwama/Balakot, nationalism wave.

Lok Sabha Election 2014 (16th). NDA: 336 (BJP 282 — first single-party majority since 1984). UPA: 60 (INC 44 — worst ever). Others: 147. Turnout: 66.4%. Anti-incumbency against UPA. Modi wave. End of UPA-II.

Lok Sabha Election 2009 (15th). UPA: 262 (INC 206). NDA: 159 (BJP 116). Others: 122. Turnout: 58.2%. Manmohan Singh re-elected. 26/11 aftermath, NREGA impact. First time UPA improved from previous election.

Lok Sabha Election 2004 (14th). UPA: 222 (INC 145). NDA: 189 (BJP 138). Others: 132. Turnout: 58.07%. Surprise UPA victory — India Shining campaign failed for NDA. Manmohan Singh became PM with Sonia Gandhi's support. Left parties provided outside support.
Lok Sabha Election 1999 (13th). NDA: 303 (BJP 182). Congress+: 136. Others: 104. Turnout: 60%. Vajpayee elected PM. Post-Kargil War wave. Stable NDA government for full 5-year term.
